confidence: Medium for source traceability; low for successful implementation from this story alone.

QA Results were not populated because this was a PO draft validation, not a QA implementation verdict.

notes: Article I CLI First requires backend/CLI-operable capability before UI emphasis.
Article II respected: PO validation only; QA Results not populated as QA verdict.
Article III enforced: story remains Draft until readiness blockers are fixed.
Article IV enforced: validation used existing source artifacts only.
Article V gates were rerun on 2026-05-05 and passed; story remains blocked for readiness, not code quality.

placeholders_remaining: TBD remains in Dev Agent Record fields and QA Results; acceptable before implementation/QA, but not evidence of readiness.

critical_issues.finding: Dev Notes cite external documents instead of pulling enough implementation context into the story.
critical_issues.impact: Dev agent must read external specs, violating the story template instruction.

critical_issues.finding: Story combines visual design, schema/RLS, API, frontend, Celery, n8n, WordPress, UGC moderation, search, alerts, Arva, Portal event integration, and TASK-RD01 through TASK-RD18.
critical_issues.impact: Tasks are too large to track and verify as one implementation-ready increment.

critical_issues.finding: Source docs name production paths apps/modules/redacao, backend/api/redacao, backend/workers/redacao, and n8n/workflows, but current repo inspection found no matching production directories.
The story uses quality_gate @qa, which aligns with Constitution Article II quality authority, while validate-next-story.md allows @architect, @dev, or @pm as quality_gate values.
critical_issues.impact: Framework validation rules conflict and should be reconciled before treating the assignment as machine-passable.

should_fix: Split the story or expand subtasks so each AC has concrete file paths, dependencies, and validation steps.

Bring Redacao technical details into Dev Notes: 8 alert types, auto-resolution rules, UGC scoring/credits, pt-BR search configuration, Celery queues/retry policy, n8n workflow names, and article.published payload.
Clarify CLI/backend-first sequence before UI work.
Specify actual test file locations and commands.

anti_hallucination.source_traceability: Main claims in the draft trace to fbr-portal-docs/06_REDACAO and DOCUMENTO_GERAL.
unverifiable_or_weak_claims: Expected production paths are cited from docs but not present in the current repository.
The story relies on external docs for essential implementation detail instead of embedding enough context.
